title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upsert, Revisions, and Deletion Semantics · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers entry identity, idempotent upsert, revisions, and deletion semantics into a changed idempotent versioned entry ingestion model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of idempotent versioned entry ingestion model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress…
objectives: Bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis.
Insert new, ignore unchanged, version changed, and quarantine conflicting entries atomically under unique constraints.
Store meaningful normalized revisions with source fetch, field diff, timestamps, and bounded retention instead of overwriting evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Entry Key Scope
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 7608 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Entry Key Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Entry Key Scope, keep Feed Go Entry Key Scope connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
